task_completion.description: Verify the 'MegaCorp Archive' folder exists under Local Folders.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the folder pane/sidebar of the email client. There should be a folder explicitly named 'MegaCorp Archive' nested under 'Local Folders'.

task_completion.description: Verify a message template exists with the correct subject.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look in the 'Templates' folder or a message composition window saved as a template. The subject line must be exactly 'Project Transition Notice'.

task_completion.description: Verify the template body contains the required transition phrases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the body text of the 'Project Transition Notice' template. It must contain the phrases 'transitioned off the MegaCorp account' and 'Please direct future inquiries to Sarah' (case-insensitive).

task_completion.description: Verify a message filter named 'MegaCorp Transition' is created and enabled with the correct condition.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Message Filters dialog. A filter named 'MegaCorp Transition' should be present and checked (enabled). In its edit window, the condition must be set to match when 'From' 'contains' '@megacorp.com'.

task_completion.description: Verify the filter has an action to reply with the created template.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the actions section of the 'MegaCorp Transition' filter edit window. There should be an action set to 'Reply with template' (or similar) pointing to the 'Project Transition Notice' template.

task_completion.description: Verify the filter has an action to forward the message to the correct email address.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the actions section of the 'MegaCorp Transition' filter edit window. There should be an action set to 'Forward Message to' with the destination address 'sarah.connor@example.com'.

task_completion.description: Verify the filter has an action to move the message to the correct folder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the actions section of the 'MegaCorp Transition' filter edit window. There should be an action set to 'Move Message to' pointing to the 'MegaCorp Archive' folder on Local Folders.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the email client's graphical user interface to configure the settings.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the actual email client (e.g., Thunderbird) windows, dialogs, and menus, rather than terminal commands or scripts editing configuration files directly.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually created the configurations within the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The required text and settings must be entered into the correct input fields within the email client's UI (folder creation dialog, message composer, filter rules dialog), not just typed into a generic text editor.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not just display the task description on screen to fake the presence of the required text.
integrity.visual_evidence: The expected phrases and emails should be seen in their functional locations (template body, filter conditions) and not merely visible in a browser window or text file containing the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected Folder: 'MegaCorp Archive' under Local Folders. Expected Template Subject: 'Project Transition Notice'. Expected Template Body Phrases: 'transitioned off the megacorp account' and 'please direct future inquiries to sarah' (case-insensitive). Expected Filter Name: 'MegaCorp Transition'. Expected Filter Condition: 'From' contains '@megacorp.com'. Expected Forward Email: 'sarah.connor@example.com'.
